sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Great progress! Clicking at (16, 24) reduced N from 5 to 4. So clicking on white squares adjacent to N crosses can remove them. The pattern seems to be:
- Each N cross is a 4x4 or similar shape
- Clicking on the white square to the right of the cross removes it
- The crosses are at columns 14, 20, 32, 44 in row 16
- I clicked (16, 24) which is to the right of the cross at (16, 20), and it removed that cross
Now I need to remove the remaining 4 crosses. Looking at the image, I can see:
- Top grid: 3 green crosses remaining (at cols 14, 32, 44)
- Bottom grid: 1 green cross (at col 44)
Let me try clicking to the right of each remaining cross to remove them.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model updated:
- Clicking white square to the right of N cross removes it (N went from 5 to 4)
- Need to click right of each remaining N cross to remove them all
- Remaining crosses at (16, 14), (16, 32), (16, 44), (52, 44)
Plan: Click to the right of each remaining N cross to remove them

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Find remaining N crosses and click to their right
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
N_nodes = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'N']

print(f"Remaining N nodes: {len(N_nodes)}")
for n in N_nodes:
    b = n['boundary']
    center_r = (min(p[0] for p in b) + max(p[0] for p in b)) // 2
    center_c = (min(p[1] for p in b) + max(p[1] for p in b)) // 2
    max_c = max(p[1] for p in b)
    print(f"  N at ({center_r}, {center_c}), right edge col={max_c}")
    # Click to the right of the cross
    target_c = max_c + 2
    print(f"  Clicking at ({center_r}, {target_c})...")
    result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': center_r, 'col': target_c}])
    
    seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
    N_nodes = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'N']
    print(f"    N: {len(N_nodes)}, done: {result.get('done')}")
    
    if result.get('done') or result.get('level_completed'):
        print("    Level completed!")
        break

# If some crosses remain, try different offsets
if len(N_nodes) > 0:
    print(f"\nStill {len(N_nodes)} N nodes remaining")
    for n in N_nodes:
        b = n['boundary']
        center_r = (min(p[0] for p in b) + max(p[0] for p in b)) // 2
        center_c = (min(p[1] for p in b) + max(p[1] for p in b)) // 2
        print(f"  N at ({center_r}, {center_c})")
        # Try different offsets
        for dc in [4, 6, 8]:
            print(f"  Trying ({center_r}, {center_c + dc})...")
            result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': center_r, 'col': center_c + dc}])
            seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
            N_nodes = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'N']
            print(f"    N: {len(N_nodes)}, done: {result.get('done')}")
            if len(N_nodes) < 4 or result.get('done'):
                break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
